i just started playing a couple of days ago, so apologies if this is a silly question. what does this mean? what are "astral reign" stages.
Is it worth getting this? I started 3 days ago. It seems like good value, you just have to wait.

Faded Legacy is the Morimens basic ruleset
Astral Reign is arc2 which introduces new rules
u can click on the letter and see the rule
for newbies like u, dont mind that Astral thingy

Before you enter a stage, if you look around the little popup, you will see either “Faded Legacy” or “Astral Reign”. Faded Legacy is Chapters 1-9, and they have those rules/gameplay in the run. Astral Reign is Chapters 10-12, and they have separate rules/gameplay for each run. Events and D-Tide, follow “Astral Reign” most of the time, but can be checked via the little popup before a fight. Furthermore, “Soulforge Amplitude” gives increase is X% of base stats, and for some Awakeners, a another passive. Soulforge is really strong for “Astral Reign” as % upgrades in base stats matter alot. “Soulforge Amplitude” only works in Arc 2.

Faded Legacy is Arc 1.
Astral Reign is Arc 2.
more important is get yourself used to Morimens's basic and mechanics

just started. pulled one copy of tawil. with the standard pulls, i read on flares spreadsheet to pull blue. but when do i pull wheels since i saw blade of titan is a god tier wheel in the standard wheel pool

1. Tawil requires at least E1 to function, E2 to be good. I would pull for E1, then get her wheel and come back to getting her to E2 if you wanna play her.
2. Flares is an experienced player, but he is a little bit biased about some things, so be sure about what u are doing <:pray:1356053116260454534> (his spreadsheet still contains a big amount of useful information though, it's just I would ask here for other opinions as well)
3. You go to wheel jail once you have your initial team started. For Aequor realm the mark is usually E3 Caecus and E2 Goliath.

thanks. looks like you also got my followup question before i even asked lol. i was going to also ask if i should save  should i save the rest of my silver for anni, try to get more copies of tawil, or try to get the wheel unseen. sounds like i will go for one more tawil and then save. regarding point 2, are there additional resources i can look at for other povs to round out my understanding then?

Well...Tawil is still a good dps. If you like her gameplay, you can invest. Generally you will need 1 team for story and 5 teams for end game mode, so you will probably have to invest in Tawil  either way. If you don't like her much, you can save and look into Anni reruns, or test results of GMurphy (upcoming unit).
Regarding point 2...like I said, his spreadsheet still contains tons of useful information, you can just ask here as well if you are unsure.

- [Stats and Realm Mechanics](<https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1pnheXD_pkjz8YGmWWlQPnw4eXr6NRm-W6267c0K1_QU/edit?slide=id.p1#slide=id.p1>), by Mythag University
- [Beginner's Guide](<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1cC7FOebdy_J2hONmVT1QUSk6SxIRTuVoI4XADVlq5JU/edit?gid=441466965#gid=441466965>), by Flares
- [How to Aliemus 101 & 102](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1387172781829128304>), by Jynn
- [Damage Formula](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1384463374213189652>), by Jynn
- [Morimens Glossary](<https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1417237861412896859>), by Mythag University
- [Morimens Timeline Megasheet](<https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1BCfu0RdP6A4SIE0XmoevGFTKp5qbilfiTViGwprpHv4/edit?usp=sharing>), by nikolazuli
There are some more resources, you can look into them as well.
I've used only Morimens Glossary and Flares spreadsheet, not sure what others consist of.

Look into this ^ and this channel

https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1304022827824844842

These are “guides” but you will need a basic understanding of the game to discern, some “dramatized” info, good and bad.

https://morimens.huijiwiki.com/wiki/%E5%94%A4%E9%86%92%E4%BD%93

https://www.kaiden.gg/morimens/characters/

These two are wikis, not guides

content: thanks! a few final questions for now before i delve in regarding pull mechanics. how many pulls on average to get a the banner unit? is there a pity mechanic? does pity carry over? im mainly asking because it would suck to pull more on tawils banner, not get her, and then have to start fresh on whatever the next banner is. i would rather accumulate the needed amount of pulls if there is no pity carried over. if pity is carried over, at least it isnt a total loss pulling if i dont get the character
You can press "details", the button is close to Realm. All the information about pulls/pities should be there. If you will still have some questions after reading it, feel free to ask.
Pity should carry over. Moreover, Tawil is in banner for...2 weeks if I'm not mistaken? You will be able to get enough of her if you play the game, there's a big income of pulls for newcomers.

Just be sure that you like her gameplay. She is also usually played in Blue Realm. If not, you can look into Castor or Pollux as well.

Either way, I would still recommend getting Tawil's Wheel. Hand Size wheels are very valuable.

i finished looking over the rules on pulling. let me know if i understood it correctly. worst case scenario you get the rate up character in 90 pulls (aka 3 sets of guaranteed ssr at 30 pulls each). i would need some math wiz's help in calculating the actual average amount of pulls it would take. pity does carry over between banners of the same type ie limited character banners share pity and limited wheel banners share their own pity too. there is no difference in doing 5 single pulls vs 1 multi pull unlike some other gacha games that give you some sort of bonus such as a guaranteed sr.

so gameplan is one more tawil, one wheel, and then save

Plus once you have one copy of Awakener/Wheel, you will be able to use a monthly dupe on it in the worst case. So having at least 1 copy of something is already very helpful, since otherwise you would have to wait quite some time for the rerun.

E1 tawil becomes playable, E2 is very good since Tawil's exalt cards for shields/dmg cost 3-4 Arithmetica, E2 gives you Prepare, which is basically making the exalt cards cost 1 Arithmetica less each turn they are in your hand.
But you can stop at E1 if you want

Or ask someone in "team-building" to help with making D-Tide teams. It would also help if you had a good understanding of team building around a Archetype/Mechanic/Realm/Awakener. Understanding what each Survival/DPS/Support/Skey Role wants. Further understanding of each Awakeners functions and scaling, plus what they desire ideally(stat wise).
Wish there was a easier way, but genuinely, understanding it via your own actions is best
remember to bring a team that can heal to enemies that apply bleed a lot
That requires them knowing which enemies apply bleed
Which is further research into understanding the game

pov: you have 390 shield but the enemy deals 391 dmg
Me when I "Weakness" into "STR Down" hence rendering the Bleed to become 200 Bleed
